macro question is uh what will disrupt
the year-end performance chase our
answers are with the US election risk
out of the way investors must turn their
attention to a far less exciting set of
macro Catalyst each week that goes by
without a significant negative revision
to the consensus growth inflation or
policy outlooks will afford Global
liquidity the scope needed to catch up
to where its leading indicator suggest
it ought to be Bitcoin 100,000 by year
end is not unreasonable we view China's
activity and property Market data at the
end of the week as having the most
potential to cause volatility given that
China is a core feature of the currently
positive and likely accelerating Global
liquidity impulse additionally fed
Governor Chris Waller on Tuesday May H
at fed balance sheet policy ending fake
QT is an option if the FED loses
economic support to continue cutting the
policy rate in the coming months
transition t42 micro dashboard here as
always a wrap up with the question from
our community this one's regarding how
to play in year end uh his says High
Skipper with two trillion moving into US
markets in the last five sessions will
fund managers take year in profits or
hold in fear uh or hold in fear of loss
I'm not sure I understand that second
part of the question but I guess the
general gist of the question is you know
do we want to be playing the uh sort of
you know the the desire of fund managers
to take profits or the desire of them to
sort of Chase performance in our view we
think that the probability that a
performance chase continues uh is much
higher than a probability of a
significant profit taking exercises that
cause let's say the S&P 500 to be down
by year end from where it is from
today's price or Bitcoin to be down uh
by year and from today's price uh what
we've observed historically you could
see this empirically generally speaking
whenever you have a Raging Bull Market
that that persist into November November
and December are typically quite
positive with November typically being
the most positive month but you tend to
see Santa Claus rallies extend
themselves in December and again we
don't play you know seasonality as a
investment strategy here at 42 macro all
of our investment recommendations for
our clients are made through either
through our systematic hiso construction
process which is a trend following
strategy on uh 60% stocks 30% gold 10%
Bitcoin uh and then we use our Trend
following elements of our process to
dial up and dial down exposure to either
maximize upside capture in bull market
and minimize downside capture in bare
markets and then we use those same Trend
following process to in our
discretionary RIS management overlay
which is a more sophisticated system
that we maintain for our institutional
and Soph at retail Trader clients and
that helps them you know sort of stay on
the right side of factor risk you know
within the uh the various factors that
comprise the Equity Credit
cryptocurrency and commodities markets
so either way both of those strategies
are systematic strategies and so as long
as those systematic strategies continue
to support uh positive upside momentum
uh into and three year in that's what we
were going to be recommending for our
clients to do it's not going to be based
on my view of seasonality or my view of
what fund managers are doing or my view
of what growth is doing or my view of
what liquidity is doing my view of what
the inflation is doing or policy's doing
or corporate profits are doing all those
things are important elements to discuss
and unpack because ultimately they give
our clients you know the a sort of
emotional confidence to make the
appropriate pivots in their portfolio
when our Trend following when our
systematic Trend following elements you
know change course and change the
direction of the uh risk that we want
our clients to be taken and asset
markets to remain on the right side of
